description: Development of 140 MW geothermal power plant at Olkaria, Hell's Gate, Naivasha. The project will add to KenGen's existing 706 MW geothermal capacity, making Olkaria the world's largest single geothermal complex.
Development of 105 MW geothermal power plant at Menengai Crater, Nakuru, in partnership with Geothermal Development Company (GDC). Part of Kenya Vision 2030 energy infrastructure programme.
Development of a dedicated cargo terminal at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, Nairobi, to handle 500,000 tonnes of cargo annually and position Kenya as East Africa's air cargo hub.
Extension of electricity grid to 500,000 additional households in rural areas of Turkana, Marsabit, Mandera, and Wajir counties under the government's universal electricity access programme.
Construction of 6 new deep-water berths at Mombasa Port to increase container handling capacity from 1.5 million to 2 million TEUs annually. Funded by JICA and AfDB.
Construction of 3 operational berths at Lamu Port as the anchor of the LAPSSET Corridor connecting Kenya, Ethiopia, and South Sudan. Includes a 32-berth deep-water port in the long term.

projectName: Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) — Mombasa to Naivasha
description: Kenya's flagship infrastructure project: 485 km SGR from Mombasa to Nairobi (Phase 1, operational 2017) and 120 km Nairobi to Naivasha (Phase 2, operational 2019). Built by China Road and Bridge Corporation.
Feasibility study for extending the SGR from Naivasha to Kisumu (Phase 2B) and eventually to Malaba on the Uganda border, completing the Northern Corridor rail link.
Upgrade of the original 1978 Mombasa–Nairobi pipeline to increase throughput capacity from 440,000 to 880,000 cubic metres per month through pump station upgrades and pipe replacement.
Extension of the petroleum pipeline from Eldoret, Kenya to Kampala, Uganda (352 km) to serve the Ugandan market and reduce road transport of petroleum products.
